title: FollowMove Properties
description:
FollowMove Properties

FollowMove Properties

The following list is a summary of properties for the FollowMove object.  The details for each property are explained in the Description field.

Property	Description

Name Property	This property can set a particular name that is assigned to objects.  When FollowMove object is created, name is assigned automatically.  For the assigned name, number is added after FollowMove such as FollowMove01.

You can change the name and up to 32 characters can be specified.  English one byte characters and underscore [ _ ] are available.  Also, you cannot use number for initial character.

Description Property	This property can set explanations for objects.  The explanation can be freely written using up to 255 characters.

Enabled Property	This property can set whether to enable the object.  When specifying True, the program executes the object.  When specifying False, the program executes the next object without executing the object.  Use this property when you want to save the sequence temporary without executing it or try with different parameters by copying objects during sequence creation.

AutoStepID Property	This property can set an automatic assignment of StepID in object execution.  When specifying True, assign StepID automatically.  When specifying False, it will be the value specified by StepID Property.  When assigning automatically, (sequence number *100 + object number) is set.  StepID is recorded to files that the force or the position during the sequence execution is recorded.  It is used to determine which interval corresponds to which object.

StepID Property	This property can set StepID during the object execution.  It is only used when AutoStepID is False.

AbortSeqOnFail Property	This property can set operations when the object fails.  When specifying True, the program ends the sequence and proceeds to the next SPEL statement if the object fails.  When specifying False, the program proceeds to the next force guide object without ending the sequence even if the object fails.  It is used when you want to continue the sequence (ex: the recovery processes are included in the sequence when the object fails.)

IOPreprocEnabled Property	This property can set I/O operations when the object starts.  When specifying True, the program executes I/O operation.  When specifying False, the program does not execute I/O operation.  I/O operations are defined by IOPreprocOutputBit Property and IOPreprocOutputStatus Property.  It is used when you want to operate the hands or peripherals before the object execution.

IOPreprocOutputBit Property	This property can set I/O operations (output bit) when the object starts.  It is only used when IOPreprocEnabled is True.

IOPreprocOutputStatus Property	This property can set output status in I/O operations when the object starts.  When specifying On, set the specified output bit to On (set to 1).  When specifying Off, set the specified output bit to Off (set to 0).  It is only used when IOPreprocEnabled is True.

MotionTrajectory Property	This property can set trajectory of the object.  When specifying Straight, move on straight line trajectory.  When specifying Arc, move on arc trajectory.

AccelS Property	This property can set the acceleration during execution of the object.  However, this set value is the acceleration for the set trajectory.  The actual acceleration is adjusted by the force control functions.

AccelR Property	This property can set the angular acceleration during execution of the object.  However, this set value is the angular acceleration for the set trajectory.  The actual angular acceleration is adjusted by the force control functions.

SpeedS Property	This property can set the speed during execution of the object.  However, this set value is the speed for the set trajectory.  The actual speed is adjusted by the force control functions.

SpeedR Property	This property can set the angular velocity during execution of the object.  However, this set value is the angular velocity for the set trajectory.  The actual angular velocity is adjusted by the force control functions.

CPEnabled Property	This property can set whether to proceed to the next object when the set trajectory enters into the slowdown zone.  When specifying True, the program starts the next object when entering into the slowdown zone.  When specifying False, the program starts the next object after the set trajectory ends.  However, if the end conditions are set and achieved, the program pauses when they are achieved and proceeds to the next force guide object.  It is used to synthesize the trajectory for multiple objects with movement.

DestType Property	This property can set the method to specify the target position of the trajectory.  When specifying RobotPoint, you can set the target position by point.  When specifying Relative, you can set the relative distance such as 10mm from the start position to X direction.

DestPoint Property	This property can set the point using for the target position of the trajectory.  It is used when specifying RobotPoint in DestType.

MidPoint Property	This property can set a relay point using for the arc trajectory.  It is used when specifying Arc in MotionTrajectory and RobotPoint in DestType.

DestRelativeX Property	For the target position, this property can set the moving amount in X direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Relative in DestType.

DestRelativeY Property	For the target position, this property can set the moving amount in Y direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Relative in DestType.

DestRelativeZ Property	For the target position, this property can set the moving amount in Z direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Relative in DestType.

DestRelativeU Property	For the target position, this property can set the rotation amount in U direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Relative in DestType.

DestRelativeV Property	For the target position, this property can set the rotation amount in V direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Relative in DestType.

DestRelativeW Property	For the target position, this property can set the rotation amount in W direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Relative in DestType.

MidRelativeX Property	For the relay position, this property can set the moving amount in X direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Arc in MotionTrajectory and Relative in DestType.

MidRelativeY Property	For the relay position, this property can set the moving amount in Y direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Arc in MotionTrajectory and Relative in DestType.

MidRelativeZ Property	For the relay position, this property can set the moving amount in Z direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Arc in MotionTrajectory and Relative in DestType.

MidRelativeU Property	For the relay position, this property can set the rotation amount in U direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Arc in MotionTrajectory and Relative in DestType.

MidRelativeV Property	For the relay position, this property can set the rotation amount in V direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Arc in MotionTrajectory and Relative in DestType.

MidRelativeW Property	For the relay position, this property can set the rotation amount in W direction of coordinate systems specified by RelativeOrient and RelativeRobotLocal.  It is used when specifying Arc in MotionTrajectory and Relative in DestType.

Fx_ControlMode Property	You can set the control mode of Fx direction in the coordinate system specified by ForceOrient of the sequence.  When specifying Disabled, the robot does not move to Fx direction since the force control functions to Fx direction are not executed.  When specifying Follow, the force control functions to Fx direction is executed as the follow mode.  In the follow mode, the robot moves so that the external force will be 0.  Therefore, when the external force is applied, the robot moves to follow it.

Fx_Firmness Property	This property set a gain that shows firmness of force control functions to Fx direction during the operation of the object.  It is used when Fx_ControlMode is Follow.  When Fx_Firmness increases, the force control function to Fx direction will be strong.  Response to changes of the force is slow, however, oscillation does not occur.  When Fx_Firmness decreases, the force control function to Fx direction will be weak.  Response to changes of the force is fast, however, oscillation is easy to occur.

Fy_ControlMode Property	You can set the control mode of Fy direction in the coordinate system specified by ForceOrient of the sequence.  When specifying Disabled, the robot does not move to Fy direction since the force control functions to Fy direction are not executed.  When specifying Follow, the force control functions to Fy direction is executed as the follow mode.  In the follow mode, the robot moves so that the external force will be 0.  Therefore, when the external force is applied, the robot moves to follow it.

Fy_Firmness Property	This property set a gain that shows firmness of force control functions to Fy direction during the operation of the object.  It is used when Fy_ControlMode is Follow.  When Fy_Firmness increases, the force control function to Fy direction will be strong.  Response to changes of the force is slow, however, oscillation does not occur.  When Fy_Firmness decreases, the force control function to Fy direction will be weak.  Response to changes of the force is fast, however, oscillation is easy to occur.

Fz_ControlMode Property	You can set the control mode of Fz direction in the coordinate system specified by ForceOrient of the sequence.  When specifying Disabled, the robot does not move to Fz direction since the force control functions to Fz direction are not executed.  When specifying Follow, the force control functions to Fz direction is executed as the follow mode.  In the follow mode, the robot moves so that the external force will be 0.  Therefore, when the external force is applied, the robot moves to follow it.

Fz_Firmness Property	This property set a gain that shows firmness of force control functions to Fz direction during the operation of the object.  It is used when Fz_ControlMode is Follow.  When Fz_Firmness increases, the force control function to Fz direction will be strong.  Response to changes of the force is slow, however, oscillation does not occur.  When Fz_Firmness decreases, the force control function to Fz direction will be weak.  Response to changes of the force is fast, however, oscillation is easy to occur.

Tx_ControlMode Property	You can set the control mode of Tx direction in the coordinate system specified by ForceOrient of the sequence.  When specifying Disabled, the robot does not move to Tx direction since the force control functions to Tx direction are not executed.  When specifying Follow, the force control functions to Tx direction is executed as the follow mode.  In the follow mode, the robot moves so that the external torque will be 0.  Therefore, when the external torque is applied, the robot moves to follow it.

Tx_Firmness Property	This property set a gain that shows firmness of force control functions to Tx direction during the operation of the object.  It is used when Tx_ControlMode is Follow.  When Tx_Firmness increases, the force control function to Tx direction will be strong.  Response to changes in the torque is slow, however, oscillation does not occur.  When Tx_Firmness decreases, the force control function to Tx direction will be weak.  Response to changes of the torque is fast, however, oscillation is easy to occur.

Ty_ControlMode Property	You can set the control mode of Ty direction in the coordinate system specified by ForceOrient of the sequence.  When specifying Disabled, the robot does not move to Ty direction since the force control functions to Ty direction are not executed.  When specifying Follow, the force control functions to Ty direction is executed as the follow mode.  In the follow mode, the robot moves so that the external torque will be 0.  Therefore, when the external torque is applied, the robot moves to follow it.

Ty_Firmness Property	This property set a gain that shows firmness of force control functions to Ty direction during the operation of the object.  It is used when Ty_ControlMode is Follow.  When Ty_Firmness increases, the force control function to Ty direction will be strong.  Response to changes in the torque is slow, however, oscillation does not occur.  When Ty_Firmness decreases, the force control function to Ty direction will be weak.  Response to changes of the torque is fast, however, oscillation is easy to occur.

Tz_ControlMode Property	You can set the control mode of Tz direction in the coordinate system specified by ForceOrient of the sequence.  When specifying Disabled, the robot does not move to Tz direction since the force control functions to Tz direction are not executed.  When specifying Follow, the force control functions to Tz direction is executed as the follow mode.  In the follow mode, the robot moves so that the external torque will be 0.  Therefore, when the external torque is applied, the robot moves to follow it.

Tz_Firmness Property	This property set a gain that shows firmness of force control functions to Tz direction during the operation of the object.  It is used when Tz_ControlMode is Follow.  When Tz_Firmness increases, the force control function to Tz direction will be strong.  Response to changes in the torque is slow, however, oscillation does not occur.  When Tz_Firmness decreases, the force control function to Tz direction will be weak.  Response to changes of the torque is fast, however, oscillation is easy to occur.

PosCheckEnabled Property

PlaneEndCond Property

PlaneNumber Property	This property can set the Plane number to be used of the end conditions related to positions.  When specifying Plane in the PlaneType property, Plane of the specified number will not be changed.  When specifying Relative in the PlaneType property, redefine a new Plane to the specified number each time the object is executed.  Therefore, note that the original setting will be lost.

PlaneRelativeOrg Property	For the end conditions related to positions, this property can specify which coordinate system sets the relative position setting Plane.  It is used when Relative is specified by PlaneType.  When specifying Base, you can set the relative position on Base coordinate system.  When specifying Local, you can specify the relative position on Local coordinate system.  The Local number is specified by PlaneRelativeRobotLocal.  When specifying Tool, you can set the relative position on Tool coordinate system.

PlaneRelativeX	For the end conditions related to positions, this property can set the relative position in X direction to set Plane.  It is used when Relative is specified by PlaneType.

X direction follows the coordinate system specified by PlaneRelativeOrg.

PlaneRelativeY	For the end conditions related to positions, this property can set the relative position in Y direction to set Plane.  It is used when Relative is specified by PlaneType.

Y direction follows the coordinate system specified by PlaneRelativeOrg.

PlaneRelativeZ	For the end conditions related to positions, this property can set the relative position in Z direction to set Plane.  It is used when Relative is specified by PlaneType.

Z direction follows the coordinate system specified by PlaneRelativeOrg.
